prompt: I'm setting up logging for my iOS app. Currently using print() everywhere. What should I use instead and why?

assertions.text: Recommends os.Logger (not OSLog, not custom logging framework, not third-party like CocoaLumberjack)
assertions.rationale: Skill prescribes os.Logger as the standard — baseline may suggest alternatives

assertions.text: Shows Logger extension pattern with subsystem = Bundle.main.bundleIdentifier and named categories
assertions.rationale: Skill provides the exact setup pattern — baseline may show inline Logger creation

assertions.text: Mentions privacy annotations as a key feature (dynamic strings redacted by default in production)
assertions.rationale: The most important Logger feature that baseline responses almost never mention

assertions.text: Explains that .debug level messages have zero cost in production (compiler optimizes away)
assertions.rationale: Skill-specific knowledge about Logger performance characteristics
